title: Brevo vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business needs.
h1: Brevo vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Brevo and Drip are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different business needs. Brevo offers a broad all-in-one CRM suite with generous free tier access, while Drip specializes in e-commerce with advanced automation and revenue tracking. This comparison will help you determine which tool best fits your marketing strategy and budget.
choose_a_if: You want a free tier with substantial contact limits (up to 100,000 subscribers)
You need multi-channel marketing including SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat in one platform
You're just starting out with email marketing and want to avoid upfront costs
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need advanced revenue attribution and tracking
You want specialized automation tools designed specifically for online retailers
You prioritize e-commerce features like onsite campaigns and detailed segmentation over pricing

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Brevo is better for general businesses and those needing multi-channel marketing at a low cost, while Drip excels for e-commerce businesses requiring deep store integration and revenue tracking. Brevo has higher overall ratings on review platforms, but Drip offers more specialized e-commerce features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or Drip?
faq.a: Brevo is significantly cheaper, offering a free tier supporting up to 100,000 subscribers with paid plans starting at $25/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious marketers, Brevo is the clear winner.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data export and import. You can export your contacts, email lists, and automation workflows from Drip and import them into Brevo. Most email marketing platforms make this transition relatively straightforward, though you may need to rebuild complex automation logic depending on feature differences.
